name: safflower oil
description: A vegetable oil used in food products as a source of unsaturated fatty acids and as a carrier for fat-soluble flavors and nutrients.
raw_text: Carthamus Tinctorius Seed Oil (Explained + Products) Products Ingredients Decode INCI Login Register Login Register Products Ingredients Decode INCI Follow us on our new Insta page » Carthamus Tinctorius Seed Oil goodie Also-called-like-this: Safflower Seed Oil What-it-does: antioxidant , emollient Irritancy: 0 Comedogenicity: 0-2 Official CosIng Information All Functions: masking, skin conditioning Description: carthamus tinctorius seed oil is the oily liquid obtained from the seeds of safflower, carthamus tinctorius l., compositae. it consists principally of the triglycerides of linoleic acid CAS #: 8001-23-8 | EC #: 232-276-5 Details The oil coming from the seeds of the yellow flowered safflower plant . Similar to other plant oils, it's loaded with nourishing and moisturizing fatty acids : it's a high linoleic acid oil (70%) and has only smaller amounts of oleic acid (11%) ( this might be great for acne-prone skin ). It also contains antioxidant vitamin E (44mg/100g alpha-tocopherol). [more] [more] [less] Something incorrect or missing? Let us know!
